text:
AS A three-year participant at Launceston Aquatic Centre, I must say how, with the current Olympics, participation at Aqua Fit has improved. We all have dreams!

Loginor signup to continue reading

My mentor is Matthew Titmus. He's a cousin of Ariane, for whom we will be blessed as she forges ahead.

I believe it was William Wordsworth who said, "No matter what lies in front of you or behind you or to one side or the other, it matters more what comes from within".

Matthew you're a "champ" too. Thanks, mate!

Rod Stone, Greens Beach

JUST watching a programme,Junior Doctors Down Under,where they are plonked into various ED on their first day. Oh how I'd love to plonk our Premier, our Health Minister and our Treasurer into our EDs for the minimum of 10 weeks that these junior doctors have to work! I wonder if the experience would give us more doctors and nurses and less stadium! Don't think the pollies would know what had hit them!

Glennis Sleurink, Launceston

AUGUST 5-11 is Homelessness Week, a time to make louder our plea to our leaders to join us in taking urgent action to end homelessness.

The theme, "Homelessness Action Now," is a call to action - every Australian deserves a safe, secure home.

It is outrageous that as wealthy as Australia is, we collectively allow the glaring inequalities in housing access to continue.

Mission Australia's new youth homelessness report revealed nearly one in ten Youth Survey 2023 participants experienced homelessness in the previous year.

More than 220,000 households are waiting for social housing and some will never get access to a stable home.

The ripple effects of homelessness are profound, especially for children and young people - having a devastating impact on their lives in adulthood.

Homelessness is usually a result of poverty, housing unaffordability and inadequate support systems.

This is why Mission Australia advocates for systemic reforms aimed at tackling the root causes of homelessness.

A $500m Homelessness Prevention Transformation Fund will help people avoid homelessness in the first place.

Substantial increases to income support and rental assistance are needed so people can afford to stay safely housed and afford life's essentials.

It's time for action to create a future where homelessness is a thing of the past in Australia.

Bart Kastrop, Mission Australia Regional Leader

FOLLOWING up onThe Sunday Examiner(August 4)'s front page leader about staffing woes at the LGH, I find it incredible that the Health Minister seems to think that borrowing staff from one hospital to run another is just good old Tassie teamwork and apparently quite acceptable!

Just how out of touch with the reality of things outside parliament is this man? We don't all live in a world of hot and cold running specialists and private hospitals!

It would do some of these people good if they were sufficiently ill to experience being ramped for hours in an ambulance because the emergency department has no beds or insufficient staff to accommodate them.

Richard Hill, Newstead

THE endless claims of a fossil fuel disinformation campaign seem to be self-defeating upon closer examination.

Obviously, the industry was confronted with the possibility that in the future, they could be replaced by renewable energy, so they went about obtaining evidence in support of, and contrary to, the idea.

In the end, they seem to have accepted the proposal, as evidenced by their since-invested heavily in renewables.

Now, with nuclear energy threatening renewables, we see an anti-nuclear campaign being waged, so devoid of reasonable argument that a need has been found to resort to nonsense. "The Simpsons" landing a multi-eyed fish, Homer juggling radioactive material, with a three-eyed koala bear tossed in for good measure.

Australia has had a nuclear reactor at Lucas Heights for over 70 years. Has anyone seen a three-eyed koala bear yet?

Max Chugg, Prospect

NORTHERN Territory Police Commissioner Mr Michael Murphy gave a significant, heartfelt, wonderfully strong, positive, and wide-ranging speech. He pledged to end racism in the Northern Territory.

We all need to focus on diffusing any incidents of racism we encounter, however subtle, whoever it is aimed at or by whom.

Jill Breen, Newnham

title: Participation at Launceston Aquatic Centre rises amid Olympics | The Examiner | Launceston, TAS
description: Olympic fever boosts Aqua Fit at Launceston Aquatic Centre as participants get inspired. Read the heartwarming local stories!
